title: INTRAVENOUS FLUID ADMINISTRATION DURING COLORECTAL SURGERY
author: Cox, Mason
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Currently no systematic protocol exists within Kaiser Permanente of Southern California regarding perioperative fluid administration for colorectal surgery. The Enhanced Recovery After Surgery (ERAS) Society has published guidelines for fluid administration in this population, consisting of Goal-Directed Fluid Therapy and Zero-Balance Fluid Therapy. The authors developed an evidence-based protocol and online education module based on current literature to educate anesthesia providers (CRNAs and Anesthesiologists) on fluid administration for patients undergoing colorectal surgery. Participants completed an assessment exam before and after completion of the education module. The effectiveness of this protocol and educational module was measured by comparing pretest scores to posttest scores. The authors found that the education module resulted in a statistically significant increase in anesthesia provider knowledge of fluid administration for patients undergoing colorectal surgery.
